intents.question: What is actually supported right now?
whyThisFirst: Start here when you need the current truthful surface and compat boundary first.
This is the fastest way to see partial vs supported without confusing builder route docs with support status.
truthReminder: partial is still partial
read-only surfaces are still narrow

intents.question: Which starter pack should I pick first?
whyThisFirst: Start here when you already know you need a builder or skill pack but have not picked the first route.
This is the narrow entry for first-hop pack selection.
truthReminder: this is a router, not a parity claim
recommended pack does not mean full compatibility

intents.question: I need to compare or filter packs before choosing.
whyThisFirst: Start here when you are comparing constraints instead of asking for one default route.
This is the right first hop for side-by-side pack truth and filtering.
truthReminder: comparison is not a recommendation engine
filters narrow truthful options only

intents.question: I want one full builder path to first success.
whyThisFirst: Start here when you want one complete route instead of hopping across multiple pages yourself.
This is the right first hop for bounded first-success paths.
truthReminder: journeys are still builder-facing and partial
journeys do not imply full host or consumer parity

intents.question: I already chose a route. How do I wire a host?
whyThisFirst: Start here once you have already chosen a pack or journey and need host wiring guidance.
This is the right first hop for host-local integration steps.
truthReminder: host playbooks do not upgrade compatibility status
wiring docs are still bounded to current landed shapes

intents.question: I need a runnable host-local example.
whyThisFirst: Start here when you want a concrete host-local shape instead of a prose playbook.
This is the right first hop for copyable example structure.
truthReminder: examples are bounded starters
examples do not imply full product-shell parity

intents.question: I need to acquire or repair a Web/Login browser session first.
whyThisFirst: Start here when the real blocker is browser/session acquisition rather than host wiring or pack choice.
This is the fastest first hop for auth-portal, browser hygiene, and service-first Web/Login recovery steps.
truthReminder: browser/session recovery is still local-first
auth-portal and runbook steps do not upgrade compatibility claims

intents.question: Which keyword claims are truthful?
whyThisFirst: Start here when the problem is outward wording, docs sync, or truthful keyword usage.
This is the right first hop for human and machine keyword truth.
truthReminder: keyword truth is not ad copy
partial must stay partial

intents.question: Which provider or lane am I even talking about?
whyThisFirst: Start here when you need the current provider and lane directory before choosing a compat or host path.
This is the right first hop for provider-scoped builder questions.
truthReminder: provider directory is a runtime catalog, not a live proof verdict
provider entry does not imply every host path is equally supported

intents.question: What can the read-only MCP surface inspect today?
whyThisFirst: Start here when the question is about the current read-only MCP surface itself.
This is the right first hop for MCP status, tool inventory, and fail-closed boundaries.
truthReminder: MCP is still partial and read-only
MCP is not an execution brain
